title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: A media live-blog coverage of Fabio Giglietto's research presentation at the International Communication Association (ICA 2026) conference in Cape Town. The blog summarizes Giglietto's work analyzing partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification changes on Facebook.
description: Media live-blog coverage of Fabio Giglietto's research presentation at the ICA 2026 conference regarding Facebook's algorithmic impacts on news URL engagement.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: An event and speaker announcement listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' to discuss future paths for computational social science after the deprecation of Meta's CrowdTangle.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable' at the International Conference on Social Media & Society 2026.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto's computational social science research regarding Facebook visibility drops for Italian parliamentarians, interpreting its constitutional implications on digital freedom of speech and expression.
description: An opinion piece analyzing digital free speech in Italy that heavily cites Fabio Giglietto's research on the impacts of Facebook's political content reduction policy.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: National news coverage explaining how Fabio Giglietto's study at the University of Urbino provoked Italian political parties to propose bipartisan regulation for big tech algorithms, highlighting how the algorithm penalized elected MPs while amplifying anti-EU extremists.
description: National news coverage highlighting how Fabio Giglietto's University of Urbino study on Facebook's algorithm prompted Italian political parties to propose bipartisan regulations for big tech.
